title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes · Changed-Stakeholder Lab
summary: An independent public-benefit team supplies different users, constraints, risks, technology, data, and failure conditions and transfers hypotheses, riskiest assumptions, and disposable prototypes into a changed ranked assumption map and prototype evidence. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of ranked assumption map and prototype evidence with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.
Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Assumption Register
steps.instruction:
lab case — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The lab variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Assumption Register.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Assumption Register, keep Assumption Register connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
